title: Domino piling
description: You are given a rectangular board of M x N squares. Find the maximum number of dominoes (2 x 1 tiles) that can be placed on the board such that each domino covers exactly two squares and no two dominoes overlap. Dominoes can be placed horizontally or vertically.
input_spec: The input contains two integers M and N separated by a space (1 <= M, N <= 16).
output_spec: Print a single integer -- the maximum number of dominoes that can be placed on the board.

routes.name: Area / 2
routes.description: Each domino covers exactly 2 squares, and the entire board can be tiled greedily, so the answer is floor(M * N / 2).

observations.title: Each domino covers exactly 2 squares
observations.description: A domino is a 2x1 tile, so it always covers exactly 2 cells of the board.

hints.nudge: How many squares does each domino cover?
hints.question: If the board has M*N squares total and each domino covers 2 squares, what is the theoretical maximum?
hints.formal: Maximum possible dominoes = floor(M * N / 2), since each domino uses 2 of the M*N available cells.

observations.title: The theoretical maximum is always achievable
observations.description: On any rectangular board, you can always tile to achieve floor(M*N/2) dominoes. No cells are wasted beyond the one leftover when M*N is odd.

hints.nudge: Can you think of a way to tile any rectangle row by row?
hints.question: If M is even, can you tile each column with vertical dominoes? If M is odd, what about the last row?
hints.formal: If M is even, pair cells in each column. If M is odd, tile the first M-1 rows as even case, then tile the last row horizontally in pairs. At most 1 cell is left uncovered.

observations.title: The formula is simply floor(M * N / 2)
observations.description: The answer requires no complex logic -- just compute M * N // 2 using integer division.

hints.nudge: Can the answer be written as a one-line formula?
hints.question: What does integer division (floor division) of M*N by 2 give you?
